segments.text:
Put inat-law is also known as conflict of laws because it deals with situations where more than one legal system may apply to any dispute which involves foreign elements.

Where there is foreign elements are these the court should determine those things - which country's law should apply which court has jurisdiction whether foreign judgementation should be recognized.

Thus there is often a conflict of laws of different countries & put inat-law provides rules to resolve the conflict.

| Difference between public & private |
| --- |
| Put inat-law public inat-law |
| Governus dispute which involves foreign elements Governmental |
| Private individuals State inat-law |
| Part of domestic law Bedy of inat- |
| of each country Bydg of inat- |

An Indian citizen marries a British citizen in London. Later dispute across regarding divorce in India. The court must determine which countries law will grow that so Lex Loci declaration will be there.

Public Int -
Treaty bto two nations regulating river water sharing is governed by the Public In law.

Issues - Whether the marriage bto Aditi & Lucas is valid under private let. law & recognized in India or not?
Essential validity of marriage -
It is generally governed by the law of dominee of the parties.
It is also concerned with
Age
Consent
Capacity to marry
Prohibited relation ships -
Aditi domicile - India
Aditi has satisfied mir age requirement under Indian law which is 18 years & she does not parent's approval as parent consent is not mandatory under Indian law.
Indian courts generally recognize foreign marriages unless they violate Indian public policy.

Therefore, Aditi's family challenge to marriage is unlikely to succeed in Indian court.
b) Resolution of disputes involving individuals across different legal jurisdictions.
b) Public international law is codified in treaties & conventions while private intercoation law is entirely unwritten.
b) The voluntary recognition of foreign laws out of mutual respect & convenience
a) The law of the place where the marriage is celebrated (lex loci celebration)
a) The country where a person resides permanent with an intention to remain.
